scoring.quality_value: cubic quality multiplied by cost efficiency
scoring.weighted: 60% cubic quality + 25% cost efficiency + 15% speed efficiency
scoring.direction: higher metric is better; rank 1 is best
scoring.cost_caveat: The native utility endpoints did not expose token usage. Cost efficiency uses a disclosed 90/10 input/output list-rate proxy; exact run USD is not reported.

decision.reason: These workload-specific recommendations identify the best measured routes for the tested output contracts. Luna was the only project-copy arm with zero defects and an aggregate panel decision of Ship, while Kimi matched production-ready session-title quality at the best observed value and speed.

limitations: The findings apply to these bounded utility functions, not coding, planning, reading, or advising.
The cost comparison is a list-rate proxy because exact usage was unavailable.
Provider and serving path are part of the treatment; these Baseten results do not transfer automatically to Fireworks.
